name: Aelinnel
description:
Aelinnel

Key Demographic: Aelinnel (Gnomes)

Captial: Keisanki

Entanglements

To the Norht East:
Valewood – The Green Gate and the star-roads connect the two; the gnomish courts watch the forest's phasing. The empire's fallen towers cast long shadows into the gnomish halls.
To the South:
Aelaerem – The halfling downs are a friendly but wary neighbor; the barrows and the fae courts have an unspoken treaty. The red thread custom is shared, not owned.
To the West:
The Dolmis Sea – Trade with Zakov and Thepyrgos; the tide-path keys are valued currency. The gnomish counting-songs are an eerie counterpoint to the Reaping's hum.
To the North:
The Valewood (deep) – The empire's fallen towers are a source of treasure and terror; the star-roads are a shortcut that demands a toll in courtesy.

Quick Reference

Tagline: Count the steps, weigh the words, and never trust a single telling.

Lady of Thorns (Fae Sovereign):

“The law here flows like water over stone – it finds its own path, and woe to those who try to dam it with paper and pretense. The earth itself will testify against the unjust. But the earth also lies – politely, and only when the moon is full.”

Charcoal-burner (Commoner):

“Our kilns tell us more than your books ever could. When the smoke curls left, the tide's with you. When it splits in three, best stay by your own hearth till it burns straight again. And never trust a court that speaks in only one voice – the silence is always the second witness.”

Genre / Mood: Dark faerie tale, moonlit groves, polite iron, and the uncanny weight of a promise unsaid.

Starting Location: A tide-shed at dawn, fog curling over anchored barges, gulls crying omens over the river's black glass. A gnomish reeve offers passage on a sealed barge: “The Green Gate opens at neap tide. What we trade there, the Dolmis will not name. And if you hear someone counting your footsteps, do not turn around – they are only making sure you still have them.”

Stone, Bough, and Bright Things

The Aelinnel do not build cities. They grow them – from granite and hawthorn, from tide-polished bone and the shadow of a laugh. Their halls thread through sea-cliffs and root-galleries, their roads are counted by antler-posts that move at night, and their doors open only when you have forgotten why you came. Here, promises have weight – not the weight of ink on parchment, but the weight of a stone dropped into a still pool. The ripples go on forever.

To the north, the Valewood breathes; to the south, the downs of Amedell where the Aelaerem tie red threads to keep the Hollow at bay. The Green Gate sometimes opens onto halfling barrows – a fact neither people speaks of aloud, because speaking aloud gives the gate permission to move.

No single crown rules the gnomish lands. Dozens of courts, seasonal pacts, and garden societies weave a shifting lattice of loyalty. Some Aelinnel keep the old fae treaties, speaking in two voices and never carrying naked iron; others barter in the Green Market with memories instead of coin, treating courtesy as a kind of lockpicking. Still others vanish into the deep woods, listening to stag-roads where the trees whisper gossip about the living. The gnomish heart is divided – and that division is the only reason the fae haven't eaten them whole.

Craft and courtesy govern every traffic between mortal keeps and the courts under the hill. Gnomish stone-singers hum bridges into alignment, their melodies keeping the Green Gate at bay. Forester-wardens hammer copper nails into thresholds because iron, they say, startles the truth. In markets under living roofs, a sentence can be folded like a napkin, and a memory can be wrapped for travel – but never opened before the second sunrise. Paths sometimes shorten for those who keep good count, and lengthen for those who sneer at patterns. The fae are never rude – but they are never, ever safe.

Prominent NPCs of Aelinnel

|p0.25|p0.3|p0.15|}

Name

Role/TitleMotivationRumored Quirk
ThornLady of Thorns, Sovereign of the Hawthorn CourtRemind mortals that courtesy is a leash, not a giftShe never repeats herself. Her silences are verdicts – and her verdicts are always polite.
Caelum ViridantGreen Knight, Warden of the Stag-RoadsTest the worthy and trap the carelessHis armour is woven from living boughs that bleed when he lies. He has never bled.
Eilidh Stone-PrinceMortal ruler of the Hall of AelinnelKeep the old pacts indexed and the fae at arm's lengthShe speaks only in conditional tense. No one has heard a declarative sentence from her lips in seven years – not even her cat.
RivenHuntsman of the Moonlit RideChase down obligations and those who flee the Green GateHis horn is carved from a fossilised scream. He only blows it when a promise is nine days old – and nine days old is when the Hollow starts counting.
TallowCharcoal-burner (commoner exemplar)Read the smoke and warn the village before the fae comeHe has never lost a sheep to the Hollow. He has lost three helpers who did not listen. Their shadows still work his kilns.
SableGreen-Market BrokerTrade truths for trinkets, and never lose the marginHer receipts are written on leaf-vein paper. They curl when the bargain is unbalanced – and also when it rains, which is often, which is why she keeps a stone in her pocket.

Names of Aelinnel

Aelinnel names are built from soft vowels (ae, ei, ui, oi) and liquid consonants (l, r, n, m) with occasional sharp endings (-k, -t, -x). Surnames are often taken from a notable grove, a tide-mark, or an ancestor's defining courtesy. They evoke the sound of water over stone and wind through hawthorn – and the silence that follows when both stop.
